source_jobs.title: Head of Academics and Learning Engineering
source_jobs.description:
Outsmart is a mission-driven, early-stage startup reimagining higher education for the age of AI by creating an entirely new collegiate experience that expands access and opportunity. Backed by $38M from top venture firms including Khosla Ventures, Forerunner, DST Global Partners, and Lightspeed, the company is led by former Duolingo executives and a founding team that also includes Emmy Award winners. With a strong culture centered around high-impact, detail-oriented team members, Outsmart is already seeing significant traction, generating 1–5 million weekly organic views on social media.

About the role

The Head of Academics & Learning Engineering will lead the design and development of Outsmart’s undergraduate courses and programs, ensuring they are world-class and designed for the AI era, working closely with our Provost.

This role will report to the CEO, oversee our team of faculty and learning engineers, including instructors and learning designers, and will work to translate the institution’s educational vision into world-class curriculum, assessments, and learning experiences.

We’re looking for someone who combines the intellectual depth of a scholar with the energy and creativity of an entrepreneur — someone who deeply understands pedagogy, learning science, and assessment, but is equally fluent in technology and AI. You’ll help define Outsmart’s learning philosophy and lead the creation of the content, curriculum, assessments, and systems that power our programs.

This role is based in Los Angeles, CA, San Francisco, CA, or Lehi, UT.

Qualities We're Seeking

 - Deep academic expertise in pedagogy and learning design

 - Vision for how undergraduate learning should evolve in the AI era — combining rigor, efficacy, accessibility, and fun

 - Demonstrated experience in the development and/or validation of standardized assessments

 - Comfort operating at the intersection of education, technology, and entrepreneurship

 - Ability to lead interdisciplinary teams of faculty and designers

 - High-energy, accountable, resourceful, and mission-driven — thrives in an early-stage environment

What you'll do

 - Define and lead Outsmart’s academic philosophy and pedagogical approach

 - Oversee undergraduate curriculum and assessment development across disciplines — ensuring rigor, alignment, and measurable learning outcomes

 - Lead the Learning Team, including faculty and instructional designers, fostering excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement

 - Work closely with our Provost to ensure accreditation and regulatory compliance and to develop and implement academic quality systems for curriculum review, learning outcomes tracking, and assessment validation

 - Collaborate with product and engineering teams to design AI-enhanced, technology-enabled learning experiences

 - Drive innovation in pedagogy, experimenting with adaptive and AI-supported instructional models

Qualifications

 - Advanced degree, preferably in Education, Learning Science, Instructional Design, Math, or related field

 - 10+ years of experience in curriculum or academic leadership, ideally in higher education or edtech

 - Demonstrated success managing interdisciplinary academic teams

 - Proven record of designing programs that combine technology, pedagogy, and measurable learning outcomes

 - Familiarity with accreditation and regulatory processes

 - Strong understanding of AI and learning technologies



Perks

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

 - Healthcare for you and your dependents (medical, dental, and vision)

 - Parental leave for any full-time employee (birthing, non-birthing, and adoptive parents)

 - 15 PTO days per year, a winter break, and 12+ paid company holidays per year

 - Remote work days

 - Lunch provided in-office daily

 - Working with super talented people who want to make a difference in the world

Outsmart is committed to building an equitable and inclusive workplace and is proud to be an equal op

source_jobs.title: Senior Applied AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Outsmart is a mission-driven, early-stage startup reimagining higher education for the age of AI by creating an entirely new collegiate experience that expands access and opportunity. Backed by $38M from top venture firms including Khosla Ventures, Forerunner, DST Global Partners, and Lightspeed, the company is led by former Duolingo executives and a founding team that also includes Emmy Award winners. With a strong culture centered around high-impact, detail-oriented team members, Outsmart is already seeing significant traction, generating 1–5 million weekly organic views on social media.



What You’ll Be Doing

In this role, you’ll be at the forefront of the AI that powers Outsmart’s applications. As an early member of the engineering team you’ll play a critical role in setting the tone for Outsmart’s technical culture including setting standards for algorithmic quality, scalability, and AI-first development. You will work on both generative and discriminative use cases, focusing on content generation at scale, gamification, benchmarking and analytics, multi-modal behavior classification from mobile signals, and continuously modernizing Outsmart’s SDLC.



Who You Are

 - 5+ years experience in hands-on AI/ML role

 - Deep and proven experience with agentic engineering

 - Experience shipping AI-first applications at scale

 - Experience using AI effectively to contribute cross-platform

 - Demonstrable sense of Product, QA, and DevX

 - Ability to work autonomously in a fast‑paced environment

What You’ll Own

 - Co-ownership of AI-first SDLC

 - MCP/ML tooling

 - Contribute to prompting standards across the engineering team

 - Define and enforce benchmarks for AI applications

 - Work with Product and Engineering leadership to define AI roadmap

 - Own end-to-end deployment and maintenance of AI systems

What Helps You Succeed

 - Craftsmanship and Extreme Ownership

 - Open-mindedness

 - Frequent, direct, honest, kind communication

 - Demonstrable adoption of AI in all aspects of work

 - Track record of shipping in an environment of high-performance

 - User obsession



Salary Range: $190K-$250K



Perks

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

 - Healthcare for you and your dependents (medical, dental, and vision)

 - Parental leave for any full-time employee (birthing, non-birthing, and adoptive parents)

 - 15 PTO days per year, a winter break, and 12+ paid company holidays per year

 - Remote work days

 - Lunch provided in-office daily

 - Working with super talented people who want to make a difference in the world

Outsmart is committed to building an equitable and inclusive workplace and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Senior Learning Designer, Science
source_jobs.description:
Outsmart is a mission-driven, early-stage startup reimagining higher education for the age of AI by creating an entirely new collegiate experience that expands access and opportunity. Backed by $38M from top venture firms including Khosla Ventures, Forerunner, DST Global Partners, and Lightspeed, the company is led by former Duolingo executives and a founding team that also includes Emmy Award winners. With a strong culture centered around high-impact, detail-oriented team members, Outsmart is already seeing significant traction, generating 1–5 million weekly organic views on social media.

What You’ll Be Doing

You'll work with a highly talented and collaborative team to develop Outsmart's math learning experience from curriculum architecture through content creation, assessment design, and learner feedback loops. You'll work closely with product managers, engineers, and fellow learning designers to build rigorous, engaging, and scalable math courses that bring joy back to learning. As an AI-native company, you'll also shape how AI tools and data insights are woven into curriculum development, content generation, and continuous improvement.

Who You Are

 - Advanced degree in a core science discipline, Science Education, or a closely related field preferred

 - 3+ years designing college level science curriculum (online or hybrid experience a plus)

 - Broad scientific fluency — comfortable stewarding courses across multiple disciplines, not just your home field

 - Proven experience building technology-enabled learning experiences in collaboration with product and engineering teams

 - Fluency with education technology and AI-assisted content workflows

 - Familiarity with science education standards and frameworks (e.g., NGSS, scientific practices, inquiry-based design)

 - Strong communicator who can translate pedagogical rationale for non-technical audiences

 - Bonus: experience with virtual labs, simulation-based learning, A/B testing educational content, or app-based course design

What You’ll Work On

 - Science curriculum end-to-end — course architecture, learning outcomes, sequencing, and mastery-based progression across biology, chemistry, physics, and earth sciences

 - Assessment strategy — formative and summative assessments that accurately measure scientific understanding and inform course iteration

 - Interactive content and tooling — virtual labs, simulations, data visualizations, and interactive notebooks that make abstract concepts tangible

 - AI-assisted content pipelines — developing and validating workflows that use AI to generate, review, and scale high-quality science content

 - Data-informed iteration — interpreting qualitative and quantitative learner data to identify gaps, improve engagement, and drive efficacy

 - Cross-functional collaboration — working with Product, Engineering, and Learning Design to ship technology-enabled science experiences on schedule

What Helps You Succeed

 - You thrive in ambiguity and are energized by building from scratch

 - You balance academic rigor with startup pragmatism — you know when to ship and iterate

 - You think like a product builder, not just a subject-matter expert — you care about the learner experience at every click

 - You take ownership without being asked and surface risks early

 - You're excited about AI's role in education and know where human judgment is non-negotiable

 - You care deeply about access and the students behind every learning outcome

Salary Range: $100K–$170K



Perks

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

 - Healthcare for you and your dependents (medical, dental, and vision)

 - Parental leave for any full-time employee (birthing, non-birthing, and adoptive parents)

 - 15 PTO days per year, a winter break, and 12+ paid company holidays per year

 - Remote work days

 - Lunch provided in-office daily

 - Working with super talented people who want to make a difference in the world

Outsmart is committed to building a

source_jobs.title: Faculty Lead & Learning Engineer - Sciences
source_jobs.description:
Outsmart is a mission-driven, early-stage startup reimagining higher education for the age of AI by creating an entirely new collegiate experience that expands access and opportunity. Backed by $38M from top venture firms including Khosla Ventures, Forerunner, DST Global Partners, and Lightspeed, the company is led by former Duolingo executives and a founding team that also includes Emmy Award winners. With a strong culture centered around high-impact, detail-oriented team members, Outsmart is already seeing significant traction, generating 1–5 million weekly organic views on social media.

About the role

The Faculty Lead & Learning Engineer - Sciences is Outsmart’s designated faculty member for undergraduate science–owning course design, development, and quality–across biology, chemistry, physics, geology, etc. (intro through advanced topics). You’ll craft an engaging, rigorous, and fun science learning experience for students while setting the standard for science teaching in the AI era.

You’ll bring the depth of an academic and the instincts of a builder. That means leveraging AI and modern tooling to design high-quality curriculum, virtual labs, and assessments at scale — and collaborating closely with learning designers, product, and engineering to bring it to fruition.

Location: Los Angeles, CA, San Francisco, CA, or Lehi, UT

Reporting: Works in close partnership with the Head of Academics & Learning Engineering and university academic leadership.

Qualities We're Seeking

 - Deep disciplinary expertise in one or more core sciences; comfortable stewarding courses across the broader science portfolio

 - Mastery of pedagogy, learning design, and assessment — and enthusiasm for AI-enabled teaching and learning

 - Builder mindset: resourceful, high energy, accountable, mission driven, student-obsessed; thrives in early-stage environments

 - Clear, direct communicator who collaborates well with designers, engineers, and product

What you'll do

 - Own the science curriculum: Design and develop courses across biology, chemistry, physics, and geology (intro → advanced) with clear learning outcomes and mastery-based progress

 - Design assessments (formative & summative)

 - Leverage AI & tooling to create interactive content (e.g., a virtual science lab) that improves engagement and efficacy

 - Collaborate with Learning Design, Product, and Engineering to ship high-quality, technology-enabled science experiences (simulations, visualizations, notebooks)

 - Support accreditation & quality: Align syllabi, policies, credit hours, and outcomes with institutional standards; contribute evidence for reviews and continuous improvement

Qualifications

 - Degree in a core science field (e.g., Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Geology) or Science Education; PhD or EdD preferred

 - 3+ years teaching or leading curriculum in undergraduate science (online or hybrid experience a plus)

 - Proven fluency with education technology and AI

 - Track record of cross-functional collaboration with designers/engineers and shipping learner-centric features



Perks

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

 - Healthcare for you and your dependents (medical, dental, and vision)

 - Parental leave for any full-time employee (birthing, non-birthing, and adoptive parents)

 - 15 PTO days per year, a winter break, and 12+ paid company holidays per year

 - Remote work days

 - Lunch provided in-office daily

 - Working with super talented people who want to make a difference in the world

Outsmart is committed to building an equitable and inclusive workplace and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Outsmart is a mission-driven, early-stage startup reimagining higher education for the age of AI by creating an entirely new collegiate experience that expands access and opportunity. Backed by $38M from top venture firms including Khosla Ventures, Forerunner, DST Global Partners, and Lightspeed, the company is led by former Duolingo executives and a founding team that also includes Emmy Award winners. With a strong culture centered around high-impact, detail-oriented team members, Outsmart is already seeing significant traction, generating 1–5 million weekly organic views on social media.



What You’ll Be Doing

You’ll work with learning, design, and engineering to build engaging, effective mobile-first learning experiences, while developing a deep understanding of our students and continuously improving the product through data and intuition. You’ll own and drive both 0→1 and 1→N initiatives, partner with leadership on product strategy and key decisions, and run fast iterations across onboarding, engagement, and retention to drive growth. You will be expected to vibe code and show your ideas, not just talk about them.



Who You Are

 - 5 - 8+ years building and shipping consumer products (mobile preferred)

 - Experience owning 0 → 1 and scaling products post-launch

 - Strong product judgment and taste for user experience

 - Track record improving engagement, retention, or growth metrics

 - Comfortable prototyping and testing ideas, not just writing specs

 - Able to work autonomously in a fast-paced, high-ambiguity environment

 - Clear communicator who can work directly with founders and small teams



What You’ll Own

 - Key product initiatives from concept to launch

 - Product features across the learning experience

 - Gamification mechanics

 - Onboarding, engagement, and retention surfaces

 - Experimentation and iteration to improve core metrics

 - Product quality and user experience across shipped features

 - Product processes and ways of working as the team scales



What Helps You Succeed

 - Strong product taste and attention to detail

 - Comfortable operating in ambiguity

 - High ownership mindset, you follow things through end-to-end

 - Ability to move quickly while maintaining a high quality bar

 - Bias toward action, you ship and learn rather than over-planning

 - Curious about users and consistently stay close to their needs

 - Willing to roll up your sleeves and do whatever it takes to move things forward

 - Eager to learn and receive feedback



Salary Range: $150K - $250K



Perks

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

 - Healthcare for you and your dependents (medical, dental, and vision)

 - Parental leave for any full-time employee (birthing, non-birthing, and adoptive parents)

 - 15 PTO days per year, a winter break, and 12+ paid company holidays per year

 - Remote work days

 - Lunch provided in-office daily

 - Working with super talented people who want to make a difference in the world

Outsmart is committed to building an equitable and inclusive workplace and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Staff Mobile Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Outsmart is a mission-driven, early-stage startup reimagining higher education for the age of AI by creating an entirely new collegiate experience that expands access and opportunity. Backed by $38M from top venture firms including Khosla Ventures, Forerunner, DST Global Partners, and Lightspeed, the company is led by former Duolingo executives and a founding team that also includes Emmy Award winners. With a strong culture centered around high-impact, detail-oriented team members, Outsmart is already seeing significant traction, generating 1–5 million weekly organic views on social media.



What You’ll Be Doing

In this role, you’ll own key features of a consumer friendly learning experience. As an early member of the engineering team, you’ll play a critical role in setting the tone for Outsmart’s technical culture including setting standards for quality, scalability, and UX. You will work closely with Outsmart’s design team to ship and maintain state-of-the-art animations and interactive experiences. You’ll be expected to ship experiences that you would want to engage with. Our applications are built for both iOS and Android using Flutter, Rive, and Unity.



Who You Are

 - 5+ years mobile development experience, with demonstrable experience using Flutter

 - Proven track record building and maintaining mobile apps at scale. 1M+ users preferred

 - Deep knowledge of performance tuning and mobile testing strategies

 - Experience writing unit/E2E tests

 - Ability to work autonomously in a fast‑paced environment

What You’ll Own

 - Own and develop new mobile features end-to-end in Flutter

 - Work closely with the Design team to ship high-quality Rive and Unity experiences

 - Own end-to-end testing for all mobile features

 - Collaborate with the broader engineering team to establish technical culture and quality standards

 - Conduct code reviews and participate in architecture design sessions

What Helps You Succeed

 - High-quality work and a deep sense of ownership over what you build

 - Curiosity and openness to new ideas, feedback, and ways of working

 - Enthusiasm for incorporating AI into everyday work

 - A track record of consistently shipping great work, even in fast-paced environments

 - A user obsession mindset



Salary Range: $175K-230K



Perks

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

 - Healthcare for you and your dependents (medical, dental, and vision)

 - Parental leave for any full-time employee (birthing, non-birthing, and adoptive parents)

 - 15 PTO days per year, a winter break, and 12+ paid company holidays per year

 - Remote work days

 - Lunch provided in-office daily

 - Working with super talented people who want to make a difference in the world

Outsmart is committed to building an equitable and inclusive workplace and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Lead Assessment Developer
source_jobs.description:
Outsmart is a mission-driven, early-stage startup reimagining higher education for the age of AI by creating an entirely new collegiate experience that expands access and opportunity. Backed by $38M from top venture firms including Khosla Ventures, Forerunner, DST Global Partners, and Lightspeed, the company is led by former Duolingo executives and a founding team that also includes Emmy Award winners. With a strong culture centered around high-impact, detail-oriented team members, Outsmart is already seeing significant traction, generating 1–5 million weekly organic views on social media.



What You’ll Be Doing

You'll manage the full assessment development lifecycle at Outsmart — from item authoring through field testing, standard setting, scoring, and reporting. You'll work closely with psychometricians, faculty, product managers, and engineers to build rigorous, scalable assessments. As an AI-forward organization, you'll also help shape how AI tools are responsibly woven into our development and QA workflows.



Who You Are

 - Master's degree (Ph.D. preferred) in educational measurement, assessment , or a related field

 - 3+ years managing operational, high-stakes assessment lifecycles end-to-end

 - Proven experience leading standard-setting studies and managing diverse SME panels

 - Experience managing external vendors for field tests and pilot studies

 - Working knowledge of validity, reliability, and fairness standards (e.g., AERA/APA/NCME)

 - Familiarity with cognitive complexity frameworks (DOK, Bloom's)

 - Strong communicator who can translate psychometric concepts for non-technical audiences

 - Bonus: knowledge of CTT, IRT, CDM, DIF,, AI-assisted item generation, or accreditation reviews



What You’ll Own

 - End-to-end assessment development operations for assigned assessments

 - SME network — recruitment, training, calibration, and quality

 - Standard-setting studies — method selection, panel facilitation, and final reporting

 - Vendor and external partner relationships, SLAs, and security protocols

 - Field testing and pilot operations

 - Scoring operations for constructed-response and performance-based assessments

 - Project timelines, risk identification, and stakeholder communication

 - Core assessment documentation — specs, blueprints, training materials, technical reports



What Helps You Succeed

 - You thrive in ambiguity and are energized by building from scratch

 - You balance psychometric rigor with startup pragmatism — you know when to ship and iterate

 - You communicate with unusual clarity across technical and non-technical audiences

 - You take ownership without being asked and surface risks early

 - You're excited about AI's role in assessment and know where validation is non-negotiable

 - You care deeply about fairness and the learners behind every score

Salary Range: $120K-$180K



Perks

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

 - Healthcare for you and your dependents (medical, dental, and vision)

 - Parental leave for any full-time employee (birthing, non-birthing, and adoptive parents)

 - 15 PTO days per year, a winter break, and 12+ paid company holidays per year

 - Remote work days

 - Lunch provided in-office daily

 - Working with super talented people who want to make a difference in the world

Outsmart is committed to building an equitable and inclusive workplace and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

notes: Early-stage startup with strong engineering focus, backed by top venture firms.
